title: Hardcoded enum string "running"/"active" in cli_image_create.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_image_create.rs
Lines: 147, 173, 196

**Current code:**
- Line 147: `Some("running")` — MachineState comparison
- Line 173: `assert_eq!(img.state, "active", ...)` — ImageState comparison
- Line 196: `Some("running")` — MachineState comparison

**Problem:** Test assertions use hardcoded string literals instead of typed `MachineState`/`ImageState` enum variants. Compile-time type safety is lost.

**Suggested fix:** Use `MachineState::Running` and `ImageState::Active` typed enum values in assertions. May require changing test struct field types from String to the enum types.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — tests should still pass.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced hardcoded strings with typed enums

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on Brand (vmapi-api)
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** VMAPI `Brand` is used in vmapi-cli as a value_enum argument (vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:63) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli`

title: CI Rust version mismatch (1.90 vs 1.92)
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml:21 — CI pins Rust 1.90 but rust-toolchain.toml specifies 1.92. Developers and CI build with different compilers. Fix: align CI to use rust-toolchain.toml version.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1B: build config & CI review. Rated HIGH.

title: Shell injection in triton env output
description: Profile values (url, account, key_id) are interpolated into shell export statements without escaping in cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:29-45. When a user runs eval "$(triton env)", a malicious profile value (e.g. from triton profile create -f untrusted.json) could execute arbitrary commands. Affects bash, fish, and PowerShell output modes. Fix: escape shell metacharacters before interpolation.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3C: triton-cli infrastructure commands review. Rated CRITICAL — must fix before merge.

title: HTTP 410 Gone handling bypasses Progenitor client with 70 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 551-620

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns HTTP 410 Gone with a Machine body (not an error) for deleted machines. Progenitor treats 410 as an error and fails to parse. Workaround: 70 lines of manual HTTP handling that bypasses Progenitor entirely, including:
- Manual `get_machine()` method with direct reqwest usage
- Custom `GetMachineError` enum with `NotFound` variant
- Bypasses Progenitor response parsing

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor response override capabilities. May require upstream feature request for non-error 4xx body handling.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — deleted machine queries should still work.
close_reason: Fixed: wrap Progenitor call, catch InvalidResponsePayload for 410 recovery

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Fixed by wrapping Progenitor get_machine() and catching InvalidResponsePayload (which surfaces when 410 Gone body can't be deserialized as Error). Reduced from 70 lines of manual HTTP/auth code to a 24-line match on the Progenitor error. Also removed cloudapi-cli (dead code, superseded by triton-cli) and switched all CLI callers to use the typed wrapper.

title: UnauthenticatedClient referenced in docs but never defined
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:36-44 — Documentation references an UnauthenticatedClient type that is never actually defined in the crate. Fix: either implement UnauthenticatedClient or update the docs to remove the reference.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2D: client wrappers review. Rated MEDIUM — misleading documentation.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on JobExecution in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 1153, 1166, 1184, 1205

JobExecution enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} in four places instead of the wire-format string.

Examples:
- Line 1153: `println!("{}: {:?}", job.uuid, job.execution);`
- Line 1166: `println!("Execution: {:?}", job.execution);`
- Line 1184: `println!("  Execution: {:?}", job.execution);`
- Line 1205: `println!("{}: {:?}", job.uuid, job.execution);`

Debug format produces "Succeeded" while wire format is "succeeded" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("{}: {}", job.uuid, enum_to_display(&job.execution));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify job execution output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: --wait hardcodes Running state (wrong for stopped instances)
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/tag.rs:333-341 — The --wait flag hardcodes waiting for Running state, which is wrong when tagging stopped instances (they remain stopped). Fix: detect current instance state and wait for the appropriate target state.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated MEDIUM — wrong wait behavior.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on MigrationState/Phase in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 1555, 1568, 1569

MigrationState and MigrationPhase enums are displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of wire-format strings.

Examples:
- Line 1555: `println!("{}: {:?} ({:?})", m.vm_uuid, m.state, m.phase);`
- Line 1568: `println!("  State: {:?}", migration.state);`
- Line 1569: `println!("  Phase: {:?}", migration.phase);`

Debug format produces "Running"/"Begin" while wire format is "running"/"begin" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", m.vm_uuid, enum_to_display(&m.state), enum_to_display(&m.phase));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify migration output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: WebSocket/channel endpoint guidance absent from AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not document how to implement or consume WebSocket/channel endpoints (changefeed, VNC, migration watch). These are non-trivial patterns. Fix: add guidance for WebSocket endpoint implementation and client consumption.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Silent serialization failure with unwrap_or_default() on to_value()
description: Both client lib.rs files (~30 locations) — serde_json::to_value() failures are handled with unwrap_or_default(), which silently produces serde_json::Value::Null instead of reporting the error. Fix: propagate the error or use expect() with a descriptive message for truly infallible cases.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2D: client wrappers review. Rated MEDIUM — silent failure pattern across ~30 locations.

title: Debug format anti-pattern not listed in Type Safety Rules
description: AGENTS.md Type Safety Rules section does not mention the {:?} Debug format anti-pattern, even though it has been a recurring issue. The rule against using Debug format for user-facing output should be documented alongside the other type safety rules. Fix: add a new rule to the Type Safety Rules section.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated HIGH — recurring anti-pattern needs to be codified.

title: DSA signature silently truncates oversized r/s components
description: libs/triton-auth/src/legacy_pem.rs:373-382 — DSA signature encoding silently truncates r and s components that exceed the expected 20-byte size. This could produce invalid signatures without any error. Fix: return an error if components exceed expected bounds.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM — silent data corruption.

title: RBAC role values not URL-encoded in query parameter
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/auth.rs:100 — RBAC role values are appended as query parameters without URL encoding. Role names containing special characters (spaces, ampersands, etc.) will produce malformed URLs. Fix: URL-encode the role value before appending.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2D: client wrappers review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: Field naming exceptions undocumented in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not document the conventions around field naming exceptions (e.g., when serde rename attributes override the default naming). Contributors may not know when/how to use rename attributes. Fix: add a section documenting field naming conventions and exception patterns.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: MigrationPhase enum missing #[serde(other)] catch-all
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/migrations.rs:87-93 — MigrationPhase only has 3 variants with no catch-all. A new migration phase from the server will cause deserialization failure. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown variant.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From reviews 2C/3A: vmapi-api types and type-safety audit. Rated HIGH — forward compat.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on VmState in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 779, 808, 1525

VmState enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string. VmState has a Display impl in apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs, but the CLI should use enum_to_display() for consistency with triton-cli.

Examples:
- Line 779: `println!("{}: {} ({:?})", vm.uuid, alias_str, vm.state);`
- Line 808: `println!("State: {:?}", vm.state);`
- Line 1525: `println!("  {}: {:?}", uuid, status.state);`

Debug format produces "Running" while wire format is "running". This is inconsistent with triton-cli output.

Suggested fix: Use enum_to_display() from triton-cli output module, or add a similar helper to vmapi-cli:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", vm.uuid, alias_str, enum_to_display(&vm.state));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on ImageState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs, lines 27-33

ImageState enum is deserialized from server responses (used in Image struct at line 143) but lacks a #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. If the server adds a new image state, deserialization will fail.

Current definition:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
}
```

Suggested fix: Add an Unknown catch-all variant:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
    /// Unknown state (forward compatibility)
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

Note: ImageState also has clap::ValueEnum derived. The Unknown variant will need #[clap(skip)] to prevent it from appearing as a CLI argument option.

Verification: Run `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` and `make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` to confirm compilation and tests pass.

title: SshIdentity::new public API lacks bounds validation
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:123-139 — SshIdentity::new is a public API that accepts raw bytes but does not validate bounds or format. Callers can create malformed identities that cause panics later during use. Fix: add validation in the constructor.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on slog::Level in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

This is the canonical anti-pattern: `format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()` — formatting an enum with Debug and calling .to_lowercase().

Code:
```rust
format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()
```

slog::Level is an external library type so enum_to_display() is not directly applicable. However, the pattern is fragile — Debug output format is not guaranteed stable.

Suggested fix: Use slog::Level display string directly if available, or define a const for the default level name string.

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver`.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on Brand in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, line 807

Brand enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string.

Code: `println!("Brand: {:?}", vm.brand);`

Debug format produces "Bhyve" while wire format is "bhyve" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("Brand: {}", enum_to_display(&vm.brand));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify brand output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Package.flexible_disk silently drops data due to serde rename mismatch
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs:54,91-92 — Package struct has #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] so serde expects flexibleDisk, but CloudAPI sends flexible_disk (snake_case). Since the field has #[serde(default)], it silently becomes None even when the API returns true. Existing tests never assert on this field. Fix: add #[serde(rename = "flexible_disk")] override on the field and add test assertion.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2B: cloudapi-api types review. Rated CRITICAL — silent data loss.

title: Certgen produces self-signed certs instead of SSH-key-signed
description: libs/triton-auth/src/certgen.rs:97-100 — Certificate generation produces self-signed certificates rather than certificates signed with the SSH key. This is a functional gap that limits the usefulness of the certgen module. Fix: implement proper SSH key signing for generated certificates.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM — functional gap.

title: Action-dispatch pattern missing from AGENTS.md
description: The action-dispatch CLI pattern (commands dispatching to sub-actions) is only documented in skill reference files, not in AGENTS.md. This pattern is central to the CLI architecture but invisible to contributors reading the main guide. Fix: add a section to AGENTS.md documenting the action-dispatch pattern.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated HIGH — key architectural pattern undocumented.

title: Hardcoded enum string "stopped"/"running"/"deleted" in cli_manage_workflow.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_manage_workflow.rs
Lines: 370, 385, 404, 530

**Current code:**
- Line 370: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "stopped", ...)`
- Line 385: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "running", ...)`
- Line 404: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "running", ...)`
- Line 530: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "deleted", ...)`

**Problem:** Test assertions compare against hardcoded string literals instead of typed `MachineState` enum variants. If variant serde names change, tests silently pass with wrong comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Change test struct `state` field from `String` to `MachineState`, or compare against `enum_to_display(MachineState::Running)` etc.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — tests should still pass with typed comparisons.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced hardcoded state strings with MachineState enum variants

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on VmState
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`
**Has:** Custom `Display` and `FromStr` implementations

**Problem:** `VmState` is used in vmapi-cli as a value_enum argument (vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:60, 86) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli`

title: Debug format {:?} in dry-run output for Brand enum
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs:236-254 — Brand enum is printed with Debug format {:?} in dry-run output, showing Rust debug representation instead of wire-format string. Fix: use enum_to_display() or Display trait for user-facing output.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated HIGH — Debug format anti-pattern.

title: AccessKey.status and credentialtype are bare Strings instead of typed enums
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/key.rs:72-89 — AccessKey struct uses bare String for status and credentialtype fields instead of typed enums. This loses type safety and forces string comparisons in consuming code. Fix: define proper enums with #[serde(other)] Unknown variants for both fields.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2B: cloudapi-api types review. Rated HIGH — type safety gap.

title: Divergent profile resolution logic between config/mod.rs and main.rs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/config/mod.rs and cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs — Profile resolution logic is duplicated between these two files with subtle differences. This can lead to different profiles being selected depending on code path. Fix: consolidate into a single resolution function.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3C: triton-cli infrastructure commands review. Rated MEDIUM — divergent logic.

title: Hardcoded enum string "failed" in rbac/apply.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs, line 797

**Current code:**
- `status: "failed".to_string()` in `ApplyChangeResult`

**Problem:** The `status` field in `ApplyChangeResult` is a `String` that takes values "ok", "failed", "skipped". These should be a typed enum.

**Suggested fix:** Define an `ApplyStatus` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum ApplyStatus {
    Ok,
    Failed,
    Skipped,
}
```
Then change `ApplyChangeResult.status` from `String` to `ApplyStatus`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded status strings with ApplyStatus enum

title: SSH agent parsing panics on malformed responses
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:46-258 — read_u32be, read_u8, read_string do unchecked array indexing. A malformed SSH agent response causes panics instead of returning errors. Fix: use checked slicing with proper error returns.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated HIGH.

title: RBAC role query param added after request signing
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/auth.rs:91-102 — RBAC role is appended as a query parameter after the request has been signed. If the server validates the full URI including query string against the signature, this causes a signature mismatch. Fix: add the role query param before signing the request.
close_reason: Moved role query param insertion before request signing

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2D: client wrappers review. Rated HIGH — potential auth signature mismatch.

title: json_stream_parse silently drops parse failures
description: cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:83-88 — json_stream_parse uses filter_map to silently drop lines that fail JSON parsing. This means test assertions can pass even when the CLI output is malformed. Fix: fail the test if any line fails to parse, or at minimum log warnings.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3D: test suite review. Rated HIGH — tests may pass incorrectly.

title: KeySource Debug leaks passphrase in cleartext
description: libs/triton-auth/src/key_loader.rs:23 — KeySource derives Debug which prints the passphrase field in cleartext. Fix: implement Debug manually to redact the passphrase field, or use a wrapper type that redacts on Debug.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated HIGH — credential leak risk.

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on Brand (cloudapi-api)
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/common.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** CloudAPI `Brand` enum is used in CLI instance creation commands but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the canonical API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch for the generated `Brand2` copy.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list on `Brand` in cloudapi-api.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added clap::ValueEnum derive to Brand enum

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VmState
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs:66
The VmState enum has no #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. It is deserialized from server responses and used extensively across the codebase.

If the server returns a new VM state, deserialization will fail. Follows the forward-compatibility pattern from changefeed.rs and MigrationState.

Suggested fix: Add Unknown catch-all variant with #[serde(other)]. Note: VmState has manual Display and FromStr impls (lines 91-127) that do exhaustive matching and must be updated to handle the new variant.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make lint

title: create_machine bypasses auth hooks (missing headers)
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:369-424 — create_machine is missing RBAC roles, x-act-as, and accept-version headers that other endpoints include via auth hooks. Fix: ensure create_machine uses the same auth hook pattern as other endpoints.
close_reason: Added missing RBAC roles, x-act-as, accept-version headers to create_machine

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2D: client wrappers review. Rated HIGH — auth bypass.

title: Stale repository structure diagram in AGENTS.md

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on MigrationState (vmapi-api)

**Problem:** VMAPI `MigrationState` is used in vmapi-cli as a value_enum argument (vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:126) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

title: Action dispatch enums missing Clone+Serialize derives
description:
Files:
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs — MachineAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs — ImageAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs — VolumeAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs — DiskAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/vmapi-api (VmAction): `Debug, Clone, Deserialize, JsonSchema` — missing Serialize

**Problem:** Action dispatch enums lack `Clone` and `Serialize` derives. While these are only used in request body deserialization (not CLI-facing), missing derives reduce testability and round-trip capability.

**Suggested fix:** Add `Clone, Serialize` to all action dispatch enums for consistency with other enum types.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api`
close_reason: Added Clone+Serialize derives to all action dispatch enums

title: Test issue: verify beads workflow

title: Unbounded allocation from SSH agent response
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:219,328 — 4-byte length field from agent response used directly for vec![0; len] allocation, allowing up to 4 GB allocation from a malicious/malformed agent. Fix: add a reasonable upper bound check on the length field.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated HIGH.

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on MachineType
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** `MachineType` is used in CLI code (instance list filtering) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the canonical API type. Currently relies on Progenitor build.rs patch in cloudapi-client for the generated copy, but the re-exported API type lacks it.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list on `MachineType` in the API crate.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`

title: Duplicate changefeed types in triton-cli (should use API types)
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/changefeed.rs:31-61 — Changefeed types are duplicated locally instead of importing from the API crate. This violates the 'no duplicate enum definitions' type safety rule. Fix: import changefeed types from cloudapi-api or via client re-exports.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3A: type-safety audit. Rated MEDIUM — violates type safety rule #4.

title: Hardcoded "unknown" fallback strings in CLI output code
description:
Files:
- cli/cloudapi-cli/src/main.rs:288,309 — `.unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".to_string())`
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:946,987 — same pattern
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs:143 — `.unwrap_or("unknown")`
- cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:94 — same pattern

**Problem:** Multiple CLI files use hardcoded `"unknown"` as fallback for optional enum values. Inconsistent with the `Unknown` variants that already exist on state enums.

**Suggested fix:** Define `const UNKNOWN_DISPLAY: &str = "unknown"` in a shared output module (e.g., `cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs`), or better yet, leverage the `Unknown` enum variants directly by making the state fields non-optional where possible.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-cli`
close_reason: Added opt_enum_to_display helper and UNKNOWN_DISPLAY constant

title: Profile name path traversal vulnerability
description: cli/triton-cli/src/config/paths.rs:106-108 — Profile names containing ../ can escape the profiles directory, potentially reading/writing arbitrary files. Fix: validate profile names to reject path separators and .. components.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3C: triton-cli infrastructure commands review. Rated HIGH — path traversal security issue.

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on ImageType
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:36
The ImageType enum is used as a CLI value_enum argument at cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:90 but does not have clap::ValueEnum derived on the canonical API type.

The build.rs patch (cloudapi-client/build.rs:46) covers the Progenitor-generated type, but the canonical API type should also have the derive for consistency with all other CLI-argument enums (MachineState, ImageState, Brand, etc.).

Suggested fix: Add clap::ValueEnum to the derive list on ImageType.

Verification: make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make lint
close_reason: Added clap::ValueEnum (plus Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq) to ImageType canonical API type

title: RBAC apply returns Ok(()) even when operations fail
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:742-810 — rbac apply collects errors during execution but still returns Ok(()) at the end. Automation tools see exit code 0 even when operations failed. Fix: return Err if any operations failed, or use a non-zero exit code.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated HIGH — incorrect exit code for automation.

title: UUID handling behavior change undocumented in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not document the UUID handling behavior change (how UUIDs are parsed, validated, or formatted). This should be documented so contributors understand the expected behavior. Fix: add documentation about UUID handling conventions.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated HIGH — behavior change needs docs.

title: Trailing-newline sensitivity in check_transforms
description: openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs:53-66 — check_transforms comparison is sensitive to trailing newlines, which can cause false check failures depending on how the spec was generated or written. Fix: normalize trailing whitespace before comparison.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1C: OpenAPI pipeline review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: CreateMachine legacy format transformation is 152 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 1109-1260

**Problem:** Node.js CloudAPI expects tags/metadata as flattened `tag.KEY=VALUE` and `metadata.KEY=VALUE` fields, but Progenitor generates nested object structures. 152 lines of manual transformation code converts between formats, including:
- `transform_progenitor_request_to_legacy()` — extracts nested fields and flattens
- `transform_create_machine_to_legacy()` — manual field-by-field construction
- Two separate methods: `create_machine()` and `create_machine_from_progenitor()`

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor `with_conversion()` for custom serialization, or implement serde custom serializer on the request type to reduce manual code.

**Verification:** Integration tests against real CloudAPI to confirm tag/metadata round-trip.
close_reason: Removed unused create_machine (API type variant) and 110-line transform_create_machine_to_legacy; renamed create_machine_from_progenitor to create_machine

title: Inconsistent delete confirmation UX (raw stdin vs dialoguer)
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs:390-401 — Some delete commands use raw stdin for confirmation while others use the dialoguer crate. This creates inconsistent UX across the CLI. Fix: standardize on dialoguer for all interactive confirmations.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3C: triton-cli infrastructure commands review. Rated MEDIUM — inconsistent UX.

title: Unsafe env var manipulation in agent tests races with parallel tests
description: libs/triton-auth/tests/agent_keys_test.rs:42-66 — Tests manipulate environment variables (SSH_AUTH_SOCK etc.) without synchronization. When tests run in parallel, env var changes in one test affect others. Fix: use serial test execution or isolated process-level env.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3D: test suite review. Rated HIGH — test flakiness risk.

title: Integration test does not verify transform pipeline
description: openapi-manager/tests/integration_test.rs — The integration test verifies spec generation but does not test the transform pipeline (patched specs). Transforms can break silently. Fix: add test cases that verify transforms are applied correctly to the generated specs.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1C: OpenAPI pipeline review. Rated HIGH — transform pipeline untested.

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
**Missing:** `Clone, Copy, clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** `VolumeState` lacks `Clone` and `Copy` (required for `clap::ValueEnum`), and lacks `clap::ValueEnum` itself. Progenitor build.rs patches the generated copy, but the canonical API type is incomplete.

**Suggested fix:** Change derives to `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, clap::ValueEnum`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on SnapshotState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** `SnapshotState` is used in CLI snapshot commands but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the canonical API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`

title: Transform silently succeeds if Error schema is missing
description: openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs:112-138 — When a transform tries to patch the Error schema but it does not exist in the spec, the transform silently succeeds without applying changes. Fix: return an error or warning when the target schema is missing.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1C: OpenAPI pipeline review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on JobExecution
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/jobs.rs:65
The JobExecution enum has no #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. It is deserialized from server responses via the Job struct (line 119).

If the server returns a new execution state, deserialization will fail. Follows the forward-compatibility pattern from changefeed.rs.

Suggested fix: Add Unknown catch-all variant with #[serde(other)].

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make lint

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MachineState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:43
The MachineState enum has an Unknown variant but lacks #[serde(other)], so it only matches the literal string "unknown" and will not catch new states the server may add.

This enum is deserialized from server responses via the Machine struct (line 84). If the server returns a new state value, deserialization will fail instead of gracefully falling back to Unknown.

Suggested fix: Add #[serde(other)] to the Unknown variant at line 52.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make lint

title: --network and --nic CLI flags can both be specified simultaneously
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs:30-87 — Both --network and --nic flags can be specified on instance create, but they are mutually exclusive concepts. No validation rejects the conflicting combination. Fix: add a conflicts_with constraint between the two flags or validate at runtime.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated MEDIUM — conflicting flags not validated.

title: MachineType enum missing #[serde(other)] catch-all
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:61-66 — MachineType only has 2 variants (Smartmachine, Virtualmachine) with no catch-all. A new machine type from the server will cause deserialization failure. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown variant.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2B: cloudapi-api types review. Rated HIGH — forward compat.

title: ImageType enum Other variant matches literal string, not unknown values
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:42-51 — ImageType has an Other variant that only matches the literal string "other", not arbitrary unknown values. A new image type from the server will cause deserialization failure. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown variant (rename existing Other to keep backward compat if needed).

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2B: cloudapi-api types review. Rated HIGH — forward compat.

title: Mixed TLS backends (reqwest=rustls, tokio-tungstenite=native-tls)
description: Workspace uses rustls for reqwest but native-tls for tokio-tungstenite. This means two TLS implementations are compiled and linked, increasing binary size and attack surface. Fix: unify on a single TLS backend (preferably rustls) across all crates.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1B: build config & CI review. Rated LOW — nice-to-have unification.

title: resolve_instance fetches all machines unfiltered
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:58-92 — resolve_instance calls list_machines without filters and scans the full result set client-side. Expensive for large accounts with many machines. Fix: use server-side name filter if available, or limit the query.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated HIGH — performance issue for large accounts.

title: Debug format {:?} on Vec in wait.rs error messages
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/wait.rs:82-94 — Vec of states is printed with Debug format {:?} in error messages, showing Rust debug representation to users. Fix: use join/map with enum_to_display() for user-facing output.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated HIGH — Debug format anti-pattern.

title: Untyped NIC state field — Option<String> should be NicState enum
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/network.rs, line 228
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs, line 155

**Current code:**
- API: `pub state: Option<String>` in Nic struct
- CLI: `"state" => nic.state == value` — raw string comparison

**Problem:** NIC state has known finite values (provisioning, running, stopped) but is modeled as `Option<String>`, losing type safety. The CLI does unvalidated string comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `NicState` enum in cloudapi-api types:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum NicState {
    Provisioning,
    Running,
    Stopped,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```
Then change `Nic.state` to `Option<NicState>`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added NicState enum, updated Nic.state field type

title: CI coverage job has no needs: dependency on validation
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml:41-72 — Coverage job runs in parallel with validation jobs (no needs: dependency). Coverage could pass while validation fails, giving a false sense of health. Fix: add needs: [validation] to the coverage job.
close_reason: wontfix: CI file has only one job (build-test-validate, 40 lines). No separate coverage job exists — the described lines 41-72 don't exist. Issue is not applicable to current codebase.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1B: build config & CI review. Rated MEDIUM.

title: vmapi-api has zero deserialization tests
description: apis/vmapi-api/ — The vmapi-api crate has no deserialization tests at all. Type definitions cannot be validated against real VMAPI responses. Fix: add response fixtures from actual VMAPI endpoints and round-trip deserialization tests.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on IssueSort
description:
File: apis/bugview-api/src/lib.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, JsonSchema`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`
**Has:** Custom `Display` impl and `as_str()` method

**Problem:** `IssueSort` is used in bugview-cli as a value_enum argument but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch in bugview-client.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=bugview-api && make package-build PACKAGE=bugview-cli`

title: Insecure password generation in RBAC apply
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:857-875 — generate_password() uses SystemTime::now().as_nanos() as its sole entropy source, which is trivially predictable. Used for newly created RBAC sub-users. Fix: use uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string() (uses OS entropy) or add getrandom crate.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated CRITICAL — insecure entropy source for passwords.

title: Untyped disk state field — Option<String> should be DiskState enum
description:
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs, line 143

**Current code:**
- `disk.state.as_deref().unwrap_or("unknown")` — raw string with hardcoded fallback

**Problem:** Disk state is `Option<String>` in the Progenitor-generated types but has known finite values. The CLI falls back to a hardcoded `"unknown"` string.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `DiskState` enum in cloudapi-api types (or reuse `SnapshotState` if the values overlap), then update the Disk struct to use it.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added DiskState enum to replace Option<String>

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MigrationState (vmapi-api)
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/migrations.rs

**Current variants:** Begin, Estimate, Sync, Paused, Switch, Aborted, RolledBack, Successful, Failed, Running
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If VMAPI adds new migration states, deserialization will fail. CloudAPI MigrationState correctly has `#[serde(other)] Unknown` but the VMAPI counterpart does not.

**Suggested fix:** Add `#[serde(other)] Unknown` variant to `vmapi_api::MigrationState`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: Beads/type-safety-audit cross-reference missing in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md mentions both Beads issue tracking and the type-safety-audit skill but does not cross-reference them. Contributors may not know that type safety findings should be tracked as Beads issues. Fix: add cross-references between the sections.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs, lines 25-30

**Current variants:** Creating, Ready, Failed, Deleting
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If CloudAPI adds new volume states in the future, deserialization will fail with an error instead of gracefully falling back. Other state enums (MachineState, SnapshotState, MigrationState, MigrationPhase) correctly use the `#[serde(other)] Unknown` pattern.

**Suggested fix:**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum VolumeState {
    Creating,
    Ready,
    Failed,
    Deleting,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: write_ssh_mpint panics on empty input (usize underflow)
description: libs/triton-auth/src/legacy_pem.rs:532 — write_ssh_mpint performs a subtraction that underflows on empty input, causing a panic. Fix: add an early return or error for empty byte slices.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2A: triton-auth library review. Rated MEDIUM — panic on edge case.

title: Restify conversion skill not mentioned in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not mention the restify-conversion skill, which is available for migrating Node.js Restify services to Rust. This skill is useful for contributors doing migration work. Fix: add a reference to the restify-conversion skill in the relevant AGENTS.md section.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Error schema patching should use Progenitor with_patch instead of patched/ directory
description:
Files:
- openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs (post-generation transform)
- clients/internal/cloudapi-client/build.rs:14-16 (reads from patched/)
- openapi-specs/patched/cloudapi-api.json (patched spec)

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns errors as `{code, message, request_id?}` but Dropshot generates `{error_code, message, request_id}` (required). Current workaround maintains a separate `openapi-specs/patched/` directory with a post-generation transform in openapi-manager.

**Suggested fix:** Use `with_patch("Error", &error_patch)` in cloudapi-client build.rs to rename `error_code` → `code` and make `request_id` optional, then read from `openapi-specs/generated/` directly. Eliminates the patched/ directory entirely.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-client && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated spec = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched spec = actual Node.js wire format. The diff is valuable migration documentation. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Won't fix: keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched = actual Node.js wire format. The diff documents the migration gap. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

title: human_size_from_bytes array index panic on extreme values
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/info.rs:101-103 — human_size_from_bytes uses an array index computed from the value magnitude. Extremely large byte values cause an out-of-bounds array index panic. Fix: clamp the index to the array bounds or handle the overflow case.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: No write timeout on SSH agent socket
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:183-188 — Write operations to the SSH agent socket have no timeout. A hung agent socket causes the CLI to block indefinitely. Fix: set a write timeout on the UnixStream.

title: Hardcoded enum string "created" in cli_snapshots.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_snapshots.rs
Lines: 223, 279

**Current code:**
- Line 223: `assert_eq!(snap.state, "created")`
- Line 279: `assert_eq!(snap.state, "created")`

**Problem:** Test assertions compare against hardcoded `"created"` string instead of `SnapshotState::Created` typed enum variant.

**Suggested fix:** Use `SnapshotState::Created` in assertions. May require changing test struct `state` field from String to SnapshotState.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`

New brands from the server will cause deserialization failures. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown variant following the changefeed.rs pattern.
